title: SHA256 Generator
shortDescription: Convert text into a SHA-256 hash and copy the result in one step.
metaDescription: Turn any text into a SHA-256 hash instantly and review the exact 64-character hexadecimal result.
intro: Paste text, and the tool returns its SHA-256 digest as a 64-character hexadecimal string.
overview:
SHA-256 is a one-way cryptographic hash function that turns text into a fixed-length fingerprint. The same input always produces the same output, while even a tiny change in the text creates a completely different hash.

This tool is useful when you need to verify that two strings match, store or compare digests, or check the exact hash of a short phrase, filename, token, or configuration value. It works with plain text as entered, so spaces, line breaks, and punctuation all affect the result.

For best results, make sure you paste the exact text you want to hash. If you are comparing two values, both must match character for character, including capitalization and hidden whitespace.
howItWorks: Enter the text you want to hash.
The SHA-256 digest is computed immediately.
Copy the 64-character hexadecimal output or change the input and hash again.

useCases.title: Check two file names or labels
useCases.description: Hash a label exactly as written to compare it against a stored digest without exposing the original string.

useCases.title: Verify a configuration value
useCases.description: Create a repeatable hash for an API key label, environment string, or setting name before saving or sharing it.

useCases.title: Detect accidental text changes
useCases.description: Re-hash a sentence after editing it to confirm whether any character, space, or line break changed.

examples.title: Hash a password note
examples.description: A simple phrase becomes a fixed SHA-256 digest suitable for comparison or storage tests.

examples.title: Hash a sentence with punctuation
examples.description: Shows that punctuation and spacing are part of the input and change the digest.

examples.title: Hash a multiline note
examples.description: Line breaks are included in the text, so the output differs from a single-line version.

faq.question: Does SHA-256 produce the same output every time for the same text?
faq.answer: Yes. An identical input always produces the same 64-character hash. If the result differs, the input is not exactly the same, often because of whitespace, line endings, or capitalization.

faq.question: Why does a tiny change create a completely different hash?
faq.answer: SHA-256 is designed so small input changes cascade into a very different digest. That behavior helps reveal whether two texts are truly identical.

faq.question: Can I hash empty text?
faq.answer: Yes. An empty string has a valid SHA-256 digest. If your result is unexpected, check whether your input field contains hidden spaces or an extra newline.

faq.question: Does the tool ignore spaces or uppercase letters?
faq.answer: No. Every character counts. A trailing space, a tab, or a letter case change will produce a different hash.
